acceptance_targets: [ch16-craft-01] From snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:203-333, the revised scene passes only if at least two separate opposition beats force Cora, Hester, or Thea to answer active resistance before August's admission lands, and each major proof beat changes room leverage instead of simply adding another confirmation to an already-settled argument.

[FBR-002] Pass if a reader can tell, from snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5952-5956 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6090-6150, exactly what paper Cora has and how she got it without contradicting chapter 15. Fail if the chapter still calls her document the true page/original ledger proof while offering no matching transfer.

findings.acceptance_test: From snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:203-333, the revised scene passes only if at least two separate opposition beats force Cora, Hester, or Thea to answer active resistance before August's admission lands, and each major proof beat changes room leverage instead of simply adding another confirmation to an already-settled argument.
findings.problem: Once Cora halts the sale, the climax sheds too much adversarial pressure. August objects only briefly, an unnamed townswoman offers one soft objection, and the scene then moves in a straight line through Hester's testimony, the window-memory, Ezra's letter, and August's admission. Because the room mostly receives each proof beat instead of resisting it, the public reckoning feels evidentiary rather than fought for at the exact point the chapter should be most dangerous.
findings.rewrite_direction: Tighten the interruption sequence in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:203-333 so each truth beat has to win ground. Add concrete pushback from at least one buyer or townsman and one procedural voice between Cora's stop at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:203 and Thea's reading at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:283-317, or trim one confirmatory device so the surviving beats carry more leverage and cost.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a reader can tell, from snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5952-5956 and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6090-6150, exactly what paper Cora has and how she got it without contradicting chapter 15. Fail if the chapter still calls her document the true page/original ledger proof while offering no matching transfer.
findings.problem: Chapter 16 opens with August having come "last evening with the true ledger page" and later lets Cora brandish that page at auction, but chapter 15 has already surrendered the original ledger leaf to Knapp. As written, the proof object teleports into Cora's apron.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:5952-5956, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6090-6103, and snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6144-6150, revise the document Cora possesses so it matches the custody chain established in chapter 15. Options are all local to this chapter: make it the deficiency waiver plus August's spoken admission, make it a copied extract or memorandum rather than the original ledger leaf, or explicitly identify it as a duplicate August brings after the off-page meeting.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into queer secrecy, erotic specificity, dust-sick bodies, economic shame, bad bargains, and the humiliations of dependence. When deciding between a tidier scene and a truer one, choose the version that preserves historical pressure and emotional contradiction even if it leaves characters compromised, unsympathetic, or exposed.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If grief, desire, debt, illness, bodily damage, or shame are abstracted at the moment they should become sensory and social, rewrite toward the concrete consequence until the scene stops hiding from itself.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: moderate-high - contractions are the baseline for speech; uncontracted forms should appear only for emphasis, social performance, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate structural rhythms across the book: survival set-piece, aftermath inventory, intimate chamber scene, bureaucratic squeeze, outdoor search, quiet communal ritual, preparation without payoff, public reckoning, and departure elegy. Vary scene counts, dialogue density, and where the turn comes from so consecutive chapters never feel built from the same dramatic machine.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plainspoken and period-credible, with agricultural, domestic, and biblical undertones used sparingly; favor exact nouns and verbs over decorative frontier lyricism.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person rotating among Cora, Thea, Dell, August, and Hester, with only brief collective widening when weather or communal memory demands it
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Supple, pressure-sensitive prose: longer weather and labor sentences that can suddenly snap short at revelation, pain, or shame.
